faq.description: Answers to the most common questions about Auditora.ai.
faq.title: Frequently asked questions

urlLimitations.question: If it only reads my public website, how can it identify real risks?
urlLimitations.answer: The initial diagnostic identifies structural and logical risks based on industry patterns and the inferred process structure. These are well-founded hypotheses, not operational findings. The precision of the analysis increases significantly when you use the guided conversation or the full platform with actual operational context.

vsChatgpt.question: How is this different from using ChatGPT to generate processes?
vsChatgpt.answer: Auditora.ai differs in three key aspects. First, methodology: every session follows the SIPOC framework with automatic coverage gap detection. Second, deliverables: diagrams generated are real BPMN 2.0, exportable to XML, not approximate representations. Third, persistence: processes, risks, and mitigations are stored, tracked, and evolve in a structured manner.

dataAccess.question: What data do you access from my website exactly?
dataAccess.answer: The text content of up to 5 public pages is analyzed: homepage, about, services, and similar. This is the same content visible to any visitor in a browser. No internal systems, APIs, databases, or protected areas are accessed.

bpmKnowledge.question: Do I need BPM or BPMN knowledge to use the tool?
bpmKnowledge.answer: No. Auditora.ai is designed so that anyone with knowledge of their business can use it. The AI formulates relevant questions in accessible language and technical notation is generated automatically. For consultants with BPM experience, the tool accelerates the documentation process. For those without technical training, the platform manages the methodological complexity.

deepening.question: Can I go deeper than the instant report?
deepening.answer: Yes. After the initial diagnostic, you can start a guided conversation with the AI. The platform asks specific questions about operations — suppliers, inputs, activities, outputs, customers — and builds a progressively more detailed analysis. The result includes a complete FMEA with failure modes and Risk Priority Number (RPN) per activity.

dataSecurity.question: Is my data secure?
dataSecurity.answer: Yes. All information is transmitted with TLS encryption and stored encrypted at rest. Anonymous sessions expire in 24 hours. Information provided is not used to train AI models. When you create an account, data is stored in your organization's private workspace with complete isolation.

accountMigration.question: What happens to my analysis if I create an account?
accountMigration.answer: All information generated during the anonymous session — process map, risk analysis, guided conversation, and deliverables — transfers automatically to the account without data loss.

vsProcessMining.question: Is this process mining?
vsProcessMining.answer: No. Process mining analyzes system logs (ERP, CRM) to discover how processes actually execute. Auditora.ai is an elicitation-based process discovery tool: it guides structured conversations to document processes and analyze risks. These are complementary approaches. Process mining requires system data; Auditora.ai works with human knowledge.

hero.title: Process discovery and analysis with proven methodology
hero.subtitle: We combine artificial intelligence with standard frameworks (SIPOC, BPMN 2.0, and FMEA) to help consultants and auditors map processes and assess risks in a structured way.
hero.subtext: Enter a URL. The AI analyzes the public context and generates an initial diagnostic with process map and risk analysis.

alt.badge: The missing tool for your practice
alt.title: Every undocumented process is a risk you're signing off on
alt.subtitle: Your clients have critical processes nobody has mapped. Auditora.ai discovers them: AI-guided SIPOC elicitation, automatic BPMN 2.0 diagrams, and FMEA matrix with severity scoring. In a fraction of the time.
alt.subtext: Enter your corporate URL. Receive a process map and risk analysis in minutes — no signup, no commitments.

problem.title: Traditional process discovery has significant limitations

card1.title: Weeks of interviews and multiple rounds of revision
card1.description: Requires weeks of interviews and multiple rounds of revision to obtain an initial process map.

card2.title: Manual diagrams become obsolete quickly
card2.description: Manual diagrams become obsolete rapidly and require constant updating.

card3.title: Process mining requires event logs and technical access
card3.description: Process mining tools require event logs and technical access that most organizations don't have available.

card4.title: Generic AI solutions lack methodological structure
card4.description: Generic AI solutions lack methodological structure and produce results that are difficult to defend.

solution.title: Auditora.ai accelerates process mapping and analysis
solution.description: The platform combines AI with proven methodologies to generate an initial diagnostic from public information and enrich it through a structured guided conversation.

diff1.title: Integrated SIPOC methodology
diff1.description: Every session follows the SIPOC framework (Suppliers, Inputs, Process, Outputs, Customers). The AI detects coverage gaps and generates contextual questions to close them.

diff2.title: Real-time BPMN 2.0 diagrams
diff2.description: The diagram is built during the session. Exportable to XML, compatible with Camunda, Signavio, and any BPMN 2.0 tool.

diff3.title: FMEA matrix with RPN per activity
diff3.description: Each activity is evaluated against potential failure modes. Severity, Occurrence, Detection, and Risk Priority Number are calculated.

howItWorks.title: Initial diagnostic from public information
howItWorks.subtitle: A structured starting point based on the organization's public web pages.

step1.title: Enter the organization's URL
step1.description: Provide the web address of the organization you want to analyze.

step2.title: The AI analyzes available public pages
step2.description: The system processes public content to identify industry, services, and operational structure.

step3.title: Generates an initial SIPOC map and risk assessment
step3.description: An initial SIPOC map and preliminary assessment of structural risks are generated.

step4.title: Prepares the foundation for deeper elicitation
step4.description: The diagnostic serves as a starting point for a more in-depth guided conversation.

howItWorks.disclaimer: This analysis is a starting point. Precision increases with the information you provide during the guided conversation.

deepDive.title: Structured elicitation through guided conversation
deepDive.subtitle: The AI formulates specific questions based on detected gaps, following SIPOC and FMEA principles. Each response enriches the model and enables a more complete analysis.
deepDive.methodDescription: The AI does not request a general process description. It formulates specific questions based on gaps detected in the SIPOC map:
deepDive.question1: "You mentioned 3 suppliers but only 1 input. Who else provides materials or information to this step?"
deepDive.question2: "Your process has 5 activities but no branching. Is there a point where the flow changes based on a condition?"
deepDive.question3: "You identified the end customer but not the internal customer. Who receives this step's output before it reaches the customer?"
deepDive.methodNote: Each question closes a specific gap in the model.

guided.label: After guided conversation
guided.analysis: Complete FMEA with RPN per activity

platform.title: Professional deliverables
platform.subtitle: The platform generates documentation ready to present to stakeholders and audit teams.

feature1.title: Exportable BPMN 2.0 diagrams
feature1.description: Diagrams generated automatically during the session. Export XML, PNG, or PDF. Compatible with Camunda, Signavio, Bizagi.

feature2.title: Complete FMEA matrix with RPN calculation
feature2.description: Each activity evaluated against potential failure modes with Severity, Occurrence, Detection, and RPN calculation.

feature3.title: Risk identification per activity
feature3.description: Risks mapped directly to each process activity, with complete traceability.

feature4.title: Stakeholder-ready reports
feature4.description: Export professional reports with process maps, risk matrices, and structured recommendations.

feature5.title: Analysis history and versioning
feature5.description: Each analysis iteration is stored with change history. Compare versions and track process evolution.

platform.ctaSubtext: Everything you generated in the free diagnostic migrates automatically to your account.

pillar1.title: SIPOC (Six Sigma)
pillar1.description: Standard framework for process mapping: Suppliers, Inputs, Process, Outputs, Customers. We automate the structure that senior consultants already use.

pillar2.title: BPMN 2.0 (OMG)
pillar2.description: Industry standard notation for business process modeling. Interoperable and exportable diagrams.

pillar3.title: FMEA (Failure Mode Analysis)
pillar3.description: Systematic methodology for identifying and prioritizing risks. We automate the calculation of Severity, Occurrence, Detection, and RPN.
